extraction.one_line_summary: BAUHAUS Philipp surprised favorites by launching early from the left at 200m, beating four French sprinters after Sunweb worked from 90km out.
tactical_patterns: Sunweb committed to sprint leadout from 90km despite only two sprint opportunities all week, revealing confidence in form.
Teams Bora/FDJ focused exclusively on Kristoff, allowing Bauhaus space to launch uncovered from left side at 200m.
Dimension Data, Katusha, FDJ collective chase maintained 47kph in hour-3, reducing 5:50 gap to catch at 20km despite frustration.

rider_signals.BAUHAUS Philipp: positive: 22-year-old immediately post-Giro showed superior sprint legs, recognized opportunity when favorites overmarked Kristoff
rider_signals.BOASSON HAGEN Edvald: negative: team overcommitted resources marking Kristoff while Bauhaus remained unmonitored from flank
rider_signals.BOONMAN Kees: neutral: targeted mountain points via breakaway for third consecutive stage, dropped partners at 25km knowing catch inevitable
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: attracted all opponent attention but failed to convert, suggesting reputation exceeded current form
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: negative: finished second despite green jersey, fourth French rider in top-5 suggesting poor positioning coordination
